name: Madhva
dates_notes: Inscription, maṭha records, near-contemporary biographies (Maṇi-Mañjarī, Madhva-Vijaya); among the best-attested medieval Indian philosophers.

dates_evidence.description: Contemporary or near-contemporary inscriptions in the Tuluva region attest his lifetime.
Manuscript colophons across the Sarva-Mūla corpus.

lineage_polemical.note: The Tattvavāda position is the principal target of Madhusūdana's Advaita-Siddhi (via Vyāsatīrtha).
Madhva's Māyāvāda-Khaṇḍana / Mithyātvānumāna-Khaṇḍana / Upādhi-Khaṇḍana triad explicitly targets the Advaitic position attributed to Śaṅkara.

core_thesis: Madhva's *Tattvavāda* is a fully realist Vedānta organised around an irreducible *taratamya* (graded hierarchy) of two ontological orders: *svatantra-tattva*, the one wholly independent reality, identified as Viṣṇu/Nārāyaṇa; and *paratantra-tattva*, all else, whose being, knowing, and acting depend asymmetrically on him. The grain of this realism is fixed by *pañca-bheda* — the five permanent differences between *Brahman* and *jīva*, *Brahman* and *jaḍa*, *jīva* and *jīva*, *jīva* and *jaḍa*, and *jaḍa* and *jaḍa* — none of which is *vyāvahārika* and none of which dissolves in *mokṣa*. Madhva's distinctive logical instrument is *viśeṣa*, a self-distinguishing relation that lets attributes be genuinely differentiated within a single substance without multiplying entities — the device by which he secures both the unity of Viṣṇu and the reality of his *guṇa*-distinctions. *Jīvas* themselves form a metaphysically real hierarchy of *mukti-yogya*, *nitya-saṃsārī*, and *tamo-yogya*; liberation is *bhakti*-mediated *aparokṣa-jñāna* of Viṣṇu, never identity with him.

engaged_works.title: Brahma-Sutra-Bhashya
engaged_works.summary: Madhva's prose *bhāṣya* on Bādarāyaṇa's *Brahma-Sūtra*, the foundational text of *Tattvavāda* and the smallest of the major Vedānta *sūtra-bhāṣyas*. Working *adhikaraṇa* by *adhikaraṇa*, Madhva re-reads the *Sūtra* against Śaṅkara and Bhāskara as a treatise on *svatantra-paratantra* dependence: *Brahman* is exclusively Viṣṇu, *jagat-kāraṇa* in a real sense, and *upādāna* only in the sense of being the locus on which the world depends, not its material constituent. Distinctive moves include the realist reading of *anandamaya* (Viṣṇu, not *jīva*), the rejection of any *upādhi*-mediated identity between *jīva* and *Brahman*, and the *taratamya* reading of the difference *sūtras*. Where Śaṅkara reads *neti neti* as eliminating distinction within *Brahman*, Madhva reads it as eliminating *defects* in *Brahman* — a small philological choice with vast systematic consequences. The *bhāṣya* is the seed-text on which the *Anuvyākhyāna*, *Tattva-Prakāśikā*, *Nyāya-Sudhā*, *Tātparya-Candrikā*, and *Parimala* successively build.

engaged_works.title: Anuvyakhyana
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored verse-commentary on the Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya.
engaged_works.summary: Madhva's verse-*vārttika* on his own *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya*, in roughly two thousand *anuṣṭubh* verses across four *adhyāyas*, written to expand, defend, and *prove* what the *Bhāṣya* asserts in compressed form. Where the *Bhāṣya* states the *Tattvavāda* reading of an *adhikaraṇa*, the *Anuvyākhyāna* mounts the full dialectical apparatus — *pūrvapakṣa* from Advaitins, Bhāskarīyas, Jainas, Buddhists, and Cārvākas, followed by *uttarapakṣa* with formal *anumāna*-structures and *śruti-yukti-anubhava* triangulation. The text is the indispensable scholastic spine of the school: every later Dvaita *ṭīkā*-tradition, beginning with Jayatīrtha's *Nyāya-Sudhā*, takes the *Anuvyākhyāna* rather than the *Bhāṣya* as its primary base-text. It contains the most developed Madhva-pen treatments of *pratibimba*-correction, *viśeṣa*, *sākṣi*-cognition, *bheda*-defence against the Advaitic mithyātva-inferences, and the *taratamya* of *jīvas*. It is to *Tattvavāda* what the *Vārttika* of Sureśvara is to *Advaita* — the school's argumentative source.

engaged_works.title: Gita-Bhashya
engaged_works.summary: Madhva's prose *bhāṣya* on the *Bhagavad-Gītā*, paired with his separate *Gītā-Tātparya-Nirṇaya* (a doctrinal-summary work). Reading the *Gītā* as a *prasthāna* of *Tattvavāda*, Madhva interprets Kṛṣṇa as Viṣṇu speaking in his own voice and the text's apparent identity-statements (e.g. *aham ātmā guḍākeśa*) as statements of *bimba-pratibimba* dependence rather than non-difference. *Karma*, *jñāna*, and *bhakti* are read as ordered means within a single soteriology whose terminus is *aparokṣa-jñāna* of Viṣṇu and whose engine is *bhakti* — never *jñāna* alone. The *bhāṣya* repeatedly draws on otherwise-unknown *Pañcarātra* and *Mahābhārata*-affiliated citations (the so-called *Madhva-quotations*), which became a major target of Appayya Dīkṣita's later polemic and a perennial item of philological controversy. Doctrinally, the work fixes the school's reading of *Gītā* 7, 9, 10, 13, and 15 — passages Śaṅkara reads in identity-mode — as cosmological-dependence passages, supplying scriptural anchorage for *svatantra-paratantra* hierarchy.

engaged_works.title: Tattvodyota
engaged_works.summary: One of the *Daśa-Prakaraṇa*, the *Tattvodyota* ("Light on the Real") is a compact polemical *prakaraṇa* in which Madhva refutes a sequence of monist and quasi-monist positions — strict Advaita, Bhāskara-style *bhedābheda*, and *upādhi*-mediated identity — by argued sequences rather than aphoristic gesture. Its central move is to show that any *aikya* between *jīva* and *Brahman* compatible with experienced difference would have to be either accidental (which trivialises identity-*śruti*), or *upādhi*-based (which collapses into Bhāskarīyas already refuted), or *vivarta*-based (which requires an unprovable *mithyātva* of the world). Madhva's deployment of the *sākṣi* (witness-consciousness as a self-illuminating, jīva-particular faculty) here is among the cleanest in the corpus; the *Tattvodyota*'s economical structure made it the standard Dvaita textbook on *aikya-bhaṅga* and the natural object of Jayatīrtha's substantial *Tattvodyota-Ṭīkā*.

engaged_works.title: Mayavada-Khandana
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored polemic against the Advaitic māyā-doctrine.
engaged_works.summary: The *Māyāvāda-Khaṇḍana*, also of the *Daśa-Prakaraṇa*, is Madhva's compact frontal assault on the Advaitic doctrine that the world is *māyika* — neither *sat* nor *asat*, *anirvacanīya*. Madhva proceeds by exhausting the disjunction: if *māyā* is *sat*, Advaita has two reals and self-refutes; if it is *asat*, it cannot produce appearances; if it is *anirvacanīya*, then either the predicate "*anirvacanīya*" is determinate (in which case *māyā* is determinate after all) or it is itself *anirvacanīya* (in which case the doctrine is unstateable). Madhva insists *mithyā* is not equivalent to *asat* — the standard target of his opponents' counter-charge — but argues that the Advaitin's *mithyātva* still requires a coherent positive characterisation he never supplies. The text is the immediate ancestor of the *Mithyātvānumāna-Khaṇḍana* (which targets the *anumāna*-form of the same doctrine) and the *Upādhi-Khaṇḍana* (which targets its *upādhi*-mediation strategy); together the three constitute Madhva's anti-Advaita triad, against which Madhusūdana's *Advaita-Siddhi* is the eventual reply.

engaged_works.title: Mithyatvanumana-Khandana
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored polemic against the Advaitic inference for mithyātva.
engaged_works.summary: Targeting the formal *anumāna* by which Advaitins infer the *mithyātva* of the world — typically of the form *vimataṃ mithyā, dṛśyatvāt, śuktirūpyavat* — the *Mithyātvānumāna-Khaṇḍana* dissects the inference *hetu* by *hetu*. Madhva charges (i) that *dṛśyatva* (perceptibility) admits a counter-instance in *Brahman*'s self-perception within Advaita itself, (ii) that the *sapakṣa* (the silver-on-shell) is itself controvertible, since the sublation of error is only the rejection of a false predication, not the ontological annihilation of a *sat-asat-vilakṣaṇa* entity, and (iii) that the inferred *mithyātva* is itself either *satya* (and so a counter-example to its own conclusion) or *mithyā* (and so the inference is self-undermining — the *bādhita-viṣayatva* problem). Each *hetu* — *dṛśyatva*, *jaḍatva*, *paricchinnatva*, *bhāvarūpatva* — is taken in turn. The text supplies the technical scaffolding that Vyāsatīrtha's *Nyāyāmṛta* will later refine into Navya-Nyāya form and that Madhusūdana will labour to overturn in *Advaita-Siddhi*.

engaged_works.title: Upadhi-Khandana
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored polemic against the upādhi-mediation strategy.
engaged_works.summary: The third panel of the anti-Advaita triad, the *Upādhi-Khaṇḍana* refutes the strategy — most fully developed in the Bhāskara/Yādava-Prakāśa families, but tacitly assumed by Advaitins in their *avaccheda*-vāda — that *jīva* is *Brahman*-as-conditioned-by-*upādhi*, with non-difference reasserted in *mokṣa* on the removal of conditioning. Madhva runs a dilemma: either the *upādhi* is real (in which case the resulting non-difference is merely verbal, since two genuinely conditioned things differ by their conditions and not by some prior identity), or it is *mithyā* (in which case the entire account presupposes a successful *mithyātva*-doctrine already refuted in the *Mithyātvānumāna-Khaṇḍana*). He further argues that *upādhi*-talk reduces either to *aupādhika-bheda* (which is just realist difference under a different name) or to a category-mistake about how *upādhis* function in Nyāya inference. The text is short, sharp, and effectively closes the regress route by which Advaitins escape the previous two *Khaṇḍanas*.

engaged_works.title: Vishnu-Tattva-Vinirnaya
engaged_works.summary: The most-cited and arguably most influential of the *Daśa-Prakaraṇa*, the *Viṣṇu-Tattva-Vinirṇaya* ("Determination of the Reality of Viṣṇu") establishes by *pramāṇa*-analysis that Viṣṇu/Nārāyaṇa, and no other devatā, is the *param-Brahman* of *śruti*. Madhva first defends the authority and scope of *śabda-pramāṇa* — including disputed Pañcarātra and *Ṛg-veda-khila* materials — against Mīmāṃsaka and Nyāya delimitations, then deploys it together with *anumāna* and *anubhava* to show that every cosmologically maximal predicate (*sarva-jña*, *sarva-śaktimat*, *sarva-niyantṛ*, *svatantra*) attaches to Viṣṇu uniquely, and that no rival reading of *Brahman* (impersonal, attribute-less, or co-instantiated by other devas) survives. The work is also Madhva's most explicit positive theology of *taratamya* among devatās, with Brahmā-Vāyu, Sarasvatī-Bhāratī, etc., placed in the dependence-hierarchy. Its citation-fabric is dense and frequently controversial; it is the object of Jayatīrtha's important *Viṣṇu-Tattva-Vinirṇaya-Ṭīkā* and a recurring locus in inter-school polemic about the identity of the *Vedānta*-Brahman.

engaged_works.title: Gita-Tatparya-Nirnaya
engaged_works.composition_dates_notes: Composed as the doctrinal-summary companion to the Gītā-Bhāṣya in Madhva's mature period.
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored as part of the Sarva-Mūla-Granthāḥ; manuscript transmission and maṭha attestation uniform.
engaged_works.summary: The doctrinal-summary companion to the *Gītā-Bhāṣya*: not a verse-by-verse re-commentary but a *tātparya-nirṇaya* establishing what the *Gītā* as a whole *purports*. Madhva selects key verses across all eighteen chapters and shows that, read against the alternative monist and *karma*-mīmāṃsaka constructions, the text's settled purport is *Tattvavāda*: Kṛṣṇa is identical with Viṣṇu/Nārāyaṇa, the *param-Brahman* of *śruti*; the *jīva* is *paratantra*, ontologically dependent on him by *bimba-pratibimba*; *karma*, *jñāna*, and *bhakti* are ordered means whose terminus is *aparokṣa-jñāna* mediated by *bhakti*; the *vibhūti*-chapter (10), the *viśva-rūpa* (11), and the *kṣetra-kṣetrajña-vibhāga* (13) are read in *taratamya*-key. The *Tātparya* is the standard internal Mādhva reference for fixing the school's reading of any disputed *Gītā* verse, and is the work most cited when Mādhvas argue against Advaitic and Viśiṣṭādvaita readings of the *Gītā*. Companion to the *Bhāgavata-Tātparya-Nirṇaya* and *Mahābhārata-Tātparya-Nirṇaya* in genre and method.

engaged_works.title: Mahabharata-Tatparya-Nirnaya
engaged_works.composition_dates_notes: Late-mature work; one of Madhva's longest and most ambitious compositions.
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored; among the longest works of the Sarva-Mūla corpus.
engaged_works.summary: An ambitious 32-chapter verse work (approximately 5,000 verses) determining the doctrinal purport (*tātparya*) of the *Mahābhārata* — Madhva's most extensive treatment of any text outside the *Brahma-Sūtra*. Working selectively through the entire epic, Madhva reads the *Mahābhārata* as a *prasthāna*-text co-equal with *Upaniṣad*, *Brahma-Sūtra*, *Gītā*, and *Bhāgavata*, an unusual and consequential elevation. The narrative is read theologically: the *Pāṇḍavas* are exhibited as *taratamya*-graded *jīvas* under Kṛṣṇa-Viṣṇu's direct *anugraha*, with Bhīma identified as the *avatāra* of Vāyu (Madhva's distinctive *vāyu-tritaya* doctrine — Hanumān, Bhīma, Madhva himself as the three *avatāras* of Mukhya-Prāṇa). The work supplies the canonical Mādhva interpretation of contested epic episodes — the *Bhīṣma-Stuti*, the Kṛṣṇa-*viśva-rūpa*, the *Anugītā* — and incorporates many otherwise-unknown citations from *Bhāratīya*-affiliated and *Pañcarātra* sources whose authenticity has been a perennial item of philological controversy. Doctrinally pivotal because it makes the *Mahābhārata* itself a Mādhva *prasthāna* rather than a merely illustrative narrative.

engaged_works.title: Tattva-Sankhyana
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored; one of the *Daśa-Prakaraṇa* of the Sarva-Mūla corpus.
engaged_works.summary: An exceptionally compact *prakaraṇa* — under thirty verses in the standard recension — enumerating the categories (*tattvas*) of *Tattvavāda* in their *taratamya*-ordered hierarchy. The text states the school's foundational ontological division: *svatantra-tattva*, the one wholly independent reality (Viṣṇu); and *paratantra-tattva*, all else, sub-divided into *cetana* (sentient: *jīva*-classes graded as *mukti-yogya*, *nitya-saṃsārī*, *tamo-yogya*, with sub-orderings of *deva*, *ṛṣi*, *manuṣya*, etc.) and *acetana* (insentient: *prakṛti*, *kāla*, *karma*, *avidyā*-as-real-veiling, the *Vedas*, the *guṇa*-hierarchy, etc.). Despite its brevity the text is doctrinally dense: every term carries weight, and the ordering is itself argument. Jayatīrtha's *Tattva-Saṅkhyāna-Ṭīkā* unfolds it into a substantial scholastic treatment. The *Tattva-Saṅkhyāna* is the canonical Mādhva ontological summary, the obligatory reference for the school's classification of beings, and the structural counterpart to the polemical *Daśa-Prakaraṇa* texts (*Tattvodyota*, *Māyāvāda-Khaṇḍana*, etc.) — providing the positive ontology those texts presuppose.

engaged_works.title: Karma-Nirnaya
engaged_works.summary: Madhva's compact *prakaraṇa* on the doctrine of *karma* — its ontological status, the *karma-jñāna* relation, the conditions for *karmaphala*, and the precise sense in which *karma* is *paratantra* on Viṣṇu's *anumati* (permission). Against the Mīmāṃsaka treatment of *karma* as a self-sufficient *apūrva*-generating mechanism, Madhva insists that all *karma-phala* arises only by Viṣṇu's *anumati*, so that *karma* is real but never autonomous: the *jīva* is *kartā* in a real sense (against the Sāṃkhya denial of agency to the *puruṣa*) but only as instrument under the supreme *kartā*. Against the Advaitic subordination of *karma* as merely preparatory and ultimately *vyāvahārika*, Madhva insists that *karma* is *pāramārthikally* real and that the *karma-yoga* taught by the *Gītā* is constitutive, not merely propaedeutic. The text also fixes the school's analysis of *adṛṣṭa*, of *prārabdha*-versus-*sañcita-karma*, and of the *karma*-conditioned trajectory of *jīvas* under *taratamya*. Jayatīrtha's *Karma-Nirṇaya-Ṭīkā* is the standard sub-commentary.

engaged_works.title: Nyaya-Vivarana
engaged_works.composition_dates_notes: Compact methodological gloss accompanying the Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya.
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored; transmitted with the Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya in the Sarva-Mūla manuscript tradition.
engaged_works.summary: A compact methodological text that lays out the *nyāya* — the *yukti*-paddhati, or method of reasoned construction — by which the *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* proceeds. Where the *Bhāṣya* itself states conclusions in compressed form and the *Anuvyākhyāna* mounts the full dialectical apparatus, the *Nyāya-Vivaraṇa* articulates the *meta*-method: how *adhikaraṇas* are to be parsed, what counts as a *viṣaya-vākya*, how *sūtras* function as *upakrama* / *upasaṃhāra* signals, and the conditions under which *śruti*, *yukti*, and *anubhava* converge. Particular attention is given to the structure of the difference-*sūtras* (those Madhva reads as fixing *bheda* against the Advaitic *aikya*-construals) and to the rules by which *upakrama* and *upasaṃhāra* fix the *tātparya* of an *adhikaraṇa* against alternative readings of intervening verses. The text is the school's own statement of its hermeneutic discipline and is presupposed by the *Anuvyākhyāna*'s formal arguments; Jayatīrtha's gloss makes it operational for later scholastic use.

engaged_works.title: Bhagavata-Tatparya-Nirnaya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored doctrinal exposition of the Bhāgavata.
engaged_works.summary: Not a verse-by-verse commentary but a *tātparya-nirṇaya* — a determination of the doctrinal purport of the *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* — selecting and glossing key verses to show that the *Bhāgavata* teaches *Tattvavāda* rather than any monist reading. Madhva treats the *Bhāgavata* as a *prasthāna*-text co-equal with *Upaniṣad*, *Brahma-Sūtra*, and *Gītā*, an unusual elevation that anticipates by three centuries the Gauḍīya use of the same text. He reads the *Bhāgavata*'s identity-locutions (*ahaṁ sarvasya prabhavaḥ*, *vāsudevaḥ sarvam iti*) as cosmological-dependence, not metaphysical-identity, claims; the *līlās* of Kṛṣṇa as ontologically real and of one piece with Viṣṇu's *svarūpa*; and *bhakti* as the unique direct cause of *aparokṣa-jñāna*. Companion to the *Mahābhārata-Tātparya-Nirṇaya*, it forms the Mādhva canonisation of the great Vaiṣṇava narrative corpora as scriptural rather than illustrative, and supplies many of the verse-citations that anchor later Mādhva and Gauḍīya devotional metaphysics.

key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
dravyaṃ karma ca kālaśca svabhāvo jīva eva ca /
yadanugrahataḥ santi na santi yadupekṣayā //
narte tvat kriyate kiñcidityāderna hariṃ vinā /
jñānasvabhāvato 'pi syānmuktiḥ kasyāpi hi kvacit //
ajñānāṃ jñānado viṣṇurjñānināṃ mokṣadaśca saḥ /
ānandadaśca muktānāṃ sa evaiko janārdanaḥ //

pada_analysis.gloss: from the very nature of knowledge

samasa_vigrahas.note: Ablative expressing causal basis: 'from the very nature of knowledge'.
Agent noun: one who gives knowledge.
Agent noun: one who gives liberation.
Agent noun: one who gives bliss.
Traditional epithet of Viṣṇu; the etymology is inherited rather than argumentative here.

karaka_structure.note: These coordinated nominatives are the subjects of santi.
Ablative of causal dependence: they exist through his favor.
Instrumental of causal non-support: they fail to exist through his disregard.
Object of kriyate in the impersonal passive statement.

key_passages.english_close: Substance, action, time, intrinsic nature, and the individual self indeed exist through his favor; they do not exist through his disregard. From statements such as 'nothing whatsoever is done without you,' [it follows that] without Hari liberation would not occur for anyone at all, even from the very nature of knowledge. Viṣṇu is the giver of knowledge to the ignorant, he is the giver of liberation to the knowers, and he alone, Janārdana, is the giver of bliss to the liberated.
key_passages.why_this_passage: This is a fair programmatic statement of Madhva's mature position because it compresses his asymmetrical dependence-ontology and his soteriology into one argument: all categories depend on Viṣṇu, and even liberating knowledge is not self-sufficient apart from his grace. In the accessible corpus for this run, the Anuvyākhyāna is the clearest directly transcribable witness to that dependence claim.
key_passages.source_edition: GRETIL plaintext transformation of sa_mAdhva-anuvyAkhyAna.xml (version 2020-07-31), local file /orcd/home/002/eeshan/philosophy/materials/primary_texts/sanskrit/vedanta/full_corpus/madhva_anuvyakhyana_gretil.txt

key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
jīveśvarabhidā caiva jaḍeśvarabhidā tathā /
jīvabhedo mithaścaiva jaḍajīvabhidā tathā /
mithaśca jaḍabhedo yaḥ prapañco bhedapañcakaḥ //
so 'yaṃ satyo hyanādiśca sādiścennāśamāpnuyāt /
dvaitaṃ na vidyata iti tasmādajñānināṃ matam //

pada_analysis.gloss: difference between the insentient and Īśvara
difference between insentient matter and jīva

samasa_vigrahas.note: The world-manifold is characterized as a set of five real differences.

karaka_structure.note: Subject of āpnuyāt in the conditional clause.
Object reached if the world of difference were beginningful.

key_passages.english_close: The difference between jīva and Īśvara, and likewise the difference between the insentient and Īśvara; the mutual difference among jīvas, and likewise the difference between insentient matter and jīva; and the mutual difference among insentient things: that world-manifold is the fivefold difference. This is real and beginningless indeed; if it had a beginning, it would come to destruction. Therefore the statement 'duality does not exist' is the view of the ignorant.
key_passages.why_this_passage: This is not merely a famous slogan-locus: it is Madhva's own compressed definition of what the world consists in, followed immediately by the claim that this differentiated manifold is real and beginningless. For a thinker whose system is organized around permanent difference, this is exactly the sort of establishing passage that deserves selection.
key_passages.source_edition: GRETIL plaintext transformation of sa_mAdhva-anuvyAkhyAna.xml (version 2020-07-31), local file /orcd/home/002/eeshan/philosophy/materials/primary_texts/sanskrit/vedanta/full_corpus/madhva_anuvyakhyana_gretil.txt

key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
śuddhaḥ sākṣī yadā siddho duḥkhitvaṃ vāryate katham /
upajīvyapramāṇaṃ tad bhedagrāhakameva hi /
ato jīveśayorbhedaḥ śrutisāmarthyasusthiraḥ //
tathāpi tu cidānandapūrvāstatsadṛśā guṇāḥ /
sārasvarūpamasyāpi muktāvapyavaśiṣyate //
ato 'bhedavadevaitāḥ śrutayaḥ pravadanti hi /
paurāṇāni ca vākyāni sādṛśyābhedasaṃśrayāt //
sādṛśyācca pradhānatvāt svātantryādapicābhidām /
āhurīśena jīvasya na svarūpābhidāṃ kvacit //

pada_analysis.gloss: made very firm by the force of scripture
because they rest on a non-difference of similarity

samasa_vigrahas.note: A pramāṇa on which other interpretive claims must depend.

karaka_structure.note: Subject of vāryate in the rhetorical question.
That which is to be excluded once the pure witness is established.
Locative indicating the state in which the similarity remains.
Coordinated nominative subject with the śrutis.
What is asserted or denied by āhuḥ.

key_passages.english_close: When the pure witness-consciousness is established, how can suffering-status be denied? That is indeed a foundational means of knowledge, one that apprehends difference; therefore the difference between jīva and Lord is made very firm by the force of scripture. Yet qualities similar to that [Lord], beginning with consciousness and bliss, remain even for this jīva in liberation as its essential form. Therefore these scriptural statements indeed speak as if of non-difference, and the Purāṇic statements as well, because they rest on a non-difference grounded in similarity. And because of similarity, predominance, and also sovereignty, they apply a Lord-designation to the jīva, but never a designation of essential identity.
key_passages.why_this_passage: This passage fairly represents Madhva because it shows how his ontology and hermeneutics lock together: difference is secured by the very pramāṇas that interpretation must presuppose, while identity-language is reassigned to similarity rather than essence. It also avoids caricature by acknowledging the real likeness of the liberated jīva to the Lord without collapsing that likeness into identity.

key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
vairāgyato bhaktidārḍhyaṃ tenopāsā yadā bhavet /
āparokṣyaṃ bhaved viṣṇoriti pādakramo bhavet //

samasa_vigrahas.note: Immediate realization, not mediate or remote cognition.

karaka_structure.note: Source from which firmness of bhakti arises.
Instrumental link: by that firmness, upāsanā occurs.
Genitive specifying whose immediate realization is attained.

verb_modality.note: All three uses carry sequential potential/gnomic force.

key_passages.english_close: From dispassion comes firmness of devotion; when thereby contemplative worship comes to be, immediate realization of Viṣṇu comes to be. Such is the sequence of the section.
key_passages.why_this_passage: This compact verse is worth selecting because it lays out the internal order of Madhva's path rather than merely praising devotion in general: vairāgya stabilizes bhakti, bhakti issues in upāsanā, and upāsanā culminates in immediate realization of Viṣṇu. That sequence is central to the school's account of how liberation is actually reached.
